c74object.name: crucible
c74object.digest: Intelligent Track Data Merging and Comparison
c74object.description: The `crucible` object is a specialized utility for managing and merging hierarchical track data. It functions by maintaining two sets of data: the "incumbent" (the current ground truth) and the "challenger" (newly arriving data). When a complete span of data is received for a track, `crucible` performs a competitive analysis. If the challenger data is deemed superior based on its "rating" property, it replaces the incumbent data. This object is designed for high-performance data processing in complex musical systems, supporting asynchronous operation via deferral and providing detailed reach and growth metrics.

inlet.description: Accepts all primary control and data messages. This includes 'clear', 'track', 'span', 'reaches', and hierarchical data selectors. Additionally, sending a symbol sets the name of the external incumbent dictionary to be used for comparisons.

Sets the local bar length in frames. This value is used for calculating song and track reaches. If set to 0 (default), the object attempts to retrieve the bar length from an external buffer~ named 'bar'.

outlet.description: Outputs processed bar data and reach updates. 1. Bar Data: [palette (symbol), track (long), bar (long), offset (float)] - emitted when a challenger wins or upon certain data updates. 2. Reach Notification: [- (symbol), track (long), reach (long), -999999.0 (float)] - emitted when a new reach is detected that doesn't exist in the incumbent.
Outputs a '1' when the object receives a 'rebar' message and starts executing the rebar functionality, and outputs a '0' immediately after the rebar functionality completes.

Outputs current reach information. 1. Song Reach: [song (symbol), reach (long)] - monitored continuously and emitted automatically by the dedicated asynchronous worker thread. 2. Track Reach: [track_id (long), reach (long)] - monitored continuously and emitted automatically by the dedicated asynchronous worker thread. 3. Minimum Bar: [min (symbol), min_bar (long)] - monitored continuously and emitted automatically by the dedicated asynchronous worker thread.
Outputs verbose diagnostic and status information. Active only when the 'log' attribute is enabled (1).

objarg.name: incumbent-dictionary
objarg.digest: Initial name for the incumbent dictionary

method.name: anything
method.description: Accepts messages in the format '[track]::[bar]::[key] [data...]'. This populates the challenger dictionary with attributes for a specific bar on a specific track. Common keys include 'rating', 'palette', 'offset', and 'span'.

method.name: track
method.digest: Set the target track for subsequent span analysis
method.description: Sets the track ID (long or symbol) to be associated with the next incoming 'span' message.

method.name: span
method.description: Expects a list of bar timestamps. This message triggers the comparison logic: `crucible` compares the challenger bars (specified by the list) against the incumbent bars. If the challenger ratings are higher, the challenger wins and the incumbent is updated.

method.name: reaches
method.digest: Report current song and track reaches
method.description: Triggers an immediate output of the current song reach, track reaches, and minimum bar values through the third outlet. If the dedicated, asynchronous monitor thread is enabled (@monitor 1), these values are also continuously monitored and emitted automatically upon any changes.

method.name: clear
method.description: Clears the challenger dictionary, all track reaches, song reach, and internal track context.

method.name: rebar
method.digest: Convert transcript to a new bar length
method.description: Converts all of the bar timestamps present in the incumbent dictionary to multiples of the specified new bar length. Re-maps scores and absolutes pairs to the rounded post-conversion bar, updates mean/rating scores, pads contiguous missing multiples, and sends an optimized visualizer update if visualization is enabled.

method.name: replace
method.description: Expects a selector and a value, for example: 'replace 4::15972::rating 1.072468'. If the key in the selector is 'rating' and the @visualize attribute is enabled, this message sends a packet to the visualizer to update the specified bar's rating in its local database. This is used for real-time visualization updates and local smartloop analysis within the visualizer.

method.name: log
method.description: Sets the `log` attribute in real time. When enabled (1), status messages regarding comparisons, dictionary operations, and reach updates are sent to the fourth outlet.

method.name: consume
method.description: Sets the `consume` attribute in real time. When enabled (1), a winning challenger span will trigger a 'deep delete' of the incumbent bars it replaces.

method.name: visualize
method.description: Sets the `visualize` attribute in real time. When enabled (1), the current state of the transcript and any new span entries are sent to the external visualization script.

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: When enabled (1), status messages regarding comparisons, dictionary operations, and reach updates are sent to the fourth outlet.

attribute.name: async
attribute.description: When enabled (1), all message parsing, dictionary operations, and span comparisons are conducted on a dedicated background worker thread. This ensures high performance and non-blocking operation relative to the Max scheduler and main thread.

attribute.name: consume
attribute.description: When enabled (1), a winning challenger span will trigger a 'deep delete' of the incumbent bars it replaces. This also cleans up any other bars that were part of the defeated incumbent spans, preventing "orphaned" data fragments in the dictionary. WARNING: This feature is unfinished and will produce unexpected behavior.

attribute.name: fill
attribute.description: When enabled (1), any song growth event in either the positive or negative direction automatically pads/fills all other lesser tracks in that direction by copying their existing bars forward or backward to match the longest track length. WARNING: This feature is unfinished and will produce unexpected behavior.

attribute.name: defer
attribute.description: When enabled (1), processing of incoming messages and span comparisons is deferred to the Main thread. This is critical for maintaining audio thread stability when dealing with large dictionary structures.

attribute.name: visualize
attribute.description: When enabled (1), the current state of the transcript and any new span entries are sent to the external visualization script via TCP on port 9999.

attribute.name: monitor
attribute.description: When enabled (1, default), a dedicated background thread continuously monitors the transcript dictionary for any changes in song reach, track reaches, or minimum bar value, and automatically outputs them on Outlet 3. When disabled (0), continuous monitoring is stopped, which allows other dedicated instances of the object to act as the primary monitor in the patch.
